slos.description: Identify and describe anatomical structures of the human body using correct terminology.
Locate structures across organ systems on models, specimens, and imaging.
Describe the structural relationships between organs and systems.
Apply anatomical knowledge to clinical and case-based scenarios.
Demonstrate accurate anatomical drawing and synthesis from memory.

days.focus: Anatomical position, body planes, directional terms, body cavities, regional terms.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 1 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on anatomical terminology.

nightly: Read the syllabus and mark all 5 lab exam dates, the midterm, and the final in your planner.
Make flashcards for the directional terms: superior, inferior, medial, lateral, proximal, distal, anterior, posterior, superficial, deep.
Draw the body in anatomical position and label the three planes from memory.

days.focus: Classification of epithelia by shape and layers, glandular epithelium.
nightly: Pre-read the connective tissue section before tomorrow.
Build a table of epithelial types: shape, layers, and one location each.
Sketch simple squamous, stratified squamous, and pseudostratified epithelium from memory.

days.focus: Connective tissue proper, cartilage, bone, and blood as connective tissue.

items.title: Application Activity: Tissue ID Challenge
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Quiz yourself: name the connective tissue found in 8 locations without looking.
Draw a labeled diagram of the four primary tissue types covered so far.
Pre-read the integumentary chapter, focus on the epidermal layers.

days.focus: Epidermal layers, dermis, accessory structures, surface markings.
nightly: Draw a cross-section of skin from memory: label all epidermal layers, the dermis, and 3 accessory structures.
Build flashcards for the skin accessory structures: hair follicle, sebaceous gland, sweat gland, nail.
Preview Week 2: skim the bone tissue section.

weeks.theme: Skeletal System I: Bone and the Axial Skeleton

days.focus: Compact vs spongy bone, bone shapes, structure of a long bone, bone markings.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 2 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on bone tissue.

nightly: Draw a long bone in longitudinal section and label the diaphysis, epiphysis, and 6 internal structures.
Make flashcards for the bone markings: process, condyle, foramen, fossa, tubercle, trochanter.
Self-quiz: compact vs spongy bone and where each is found.

days.focus: Cranial and facial bones, sutures, major foramina, the bony orbit.
nightly: Label a blank skull diagram (lateral and anterior views) from memory.
Make flashcards for the 8 cranial and 14 facial bones.
List the bones that form the orbit and the nasal septum.

days.topic: Axial Skeleton: Vertebral Column and Thorax
days.focus: Vertebral regions, intervertebral structure, ribs, sternum, spinal curvatures.

items.title: Application Activity: Axial Skeleton Build
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Draw the vertebral column and label all five regions and both curvatures.
Compare a cervical, thoracic, and lumbar vertebra in a 3-column table.
Begin Lab Exam 1 review: re-walk every station topic from Weeks 1 and 2.

days.focus: Practical exam: foundations, histology, integumentary, axial skeleton.

items.title: Lab Exam 1
items.note: Covers Weeks 1 to 2: terminology, tissues, integument, axial skeleton.

nightly: Light night: review your Lab Exam 1 feedback once it posts.
Pre-read the upper limb section for tomorrow.
Reset your flashcard deck and retire the cards you have mastered.

weeks.theme: Skeletal System II: Appendicular Skeleton and Joints

days.focus: Pectoral girdle, humerus, radius, ulna, carpals, metacarpals, phalanges.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 3 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on the appendicular skeleton.

nightly: Label the bones of the upper limb and pectoral girdle from memory.
Make flashcards for the bony landmarks of the humerus, radius, and ulna.
Sketch the carpal bones in two rows and name each one.

days.focus: Pelvic girdle, femur, patella, tibia, fibula, tarsals, metatarsals, phalanges.
nightly: Label the bones of the lower limb and pelvic girdle from memory.
Compare the male and female pelvis in a quick labeled sketch.
Self-quiz: the tarsal bones and the arches of the foot.

days.focus: Structural and functional joint classes, synovial joint anatomy, joint movements.

items.title: Application Activity: Joint Classification
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Build a table of synovial joint types with one example and its movements for each.
Draw a synovial joint in cross-section and label 5 structures.
Lab Exam 2 review: re-walk every upper limb, lower limb, and joint station.

days.focus: Practical exam: appendicular skeleton and joints.

items.title: Lab Exam 2
items.note: Covers Week 3: appendicular skeleton and joints.

nightly: Light night: review your Lab Exam 2 feedback once it posts.
Pre-read the skeletal muscle organization section.
Update your flashcard deck before the muscular unit begins.

days.focus: Gross muscle structure, fascicle arrangement, muscle naming criteria, action vocabulary.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 4 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on skeletal muscle organization.

nightly: Draw skeletal muscle from gross structure down to the fascicle and label each connective tissue layer.
Make flashcards for the muscle naming criteria with one example muscle each.
Self-quiz the action vocabulary: flexion, extension, abduction, adduction, rotation, circumduction.

days.topic: Muscles of the Head, Neck, and Trunk
days.focus: Facial expression, mastication, neck, abdominal wall, and deep back muscles.
nightly: Label the muscles of facial expression and mastication from memory.
Sketch the abdominal wall layers and name each muscle.
Make flashcards for the origin region and action of 10 trunk muscles.

days.focus: Upper and lower limb muscle groups and compartments.

items.title: Application Activity: Limb Muscle Map
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Map the muscle compartments of the arm and leg in a labeled diagram.
Midterm review: build a one-page concept map of Weeks 1 to 4.
Re-test yourself on every flashcard deck from Weeks 1 to 4.

days.focus: Comprehensive lecture and lab exam covering Weeks 1 to 4.

items.title: Midterm Exam
items.note: Cumulative: foundations through the muscular system.

nightly: Light night: rest, and review your midterm feedback once it posts.
Pre-read the nervous tissue section.
Note any topics from the midterm that you want to revisit.

weeks.theme: Nervous System I: Tissue and Central Nervous System

days.focus: Neuron structure, neuroglia of the CNS and PNS, gray vs white matter.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 5 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on nervous tissue.

nightly: Draw a multipolar neuron and label every structure plus the direction of signal travel.
Make flashcards for the neuroglia of the CNS and the PNS.
Self-quiz: gray matter vs white matter, location and composition.

days.focus: Spinal cord cross-section, meninges, spinal nerves, plexuses, reflex arc anatomy.
nightly: Draw a spinal cord cross-section and label the horns, columns, roots, and meninges.
Map the four major nerve plexuses and name one nerve from each.
Make flashcards for the components of a reflex arc.

days.focus: Cerebrum, diencephalon, brainstem, cerebellum, ventricular system.

items.title: Application Activity: Brain Atlas Build
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Label a midsagittal brain diagram from memory.
Make flashcards for the four ventricles and the lobes of the cerebrum.
Lab Exam 3 review: re-walk the muscular and nervous tissue / CNS stations.

days.focus: Practical exam: muscular system, nervous tissue, and the central nervous system.

items.title: Lab Exam 3
items.note: Covers Weeks 4 to 5: muscular system and central nervous system.

nightly: Light night: review your Lab Exam 3 feedback once it posts.
Pre-read the cranial nerves section.
Refresh your flashcard deck before the special senses unit.

weeks.theme: Nervous System II: Peripheral Pathways and Special Senses

days.topic: Cranial Nerves and the Autonomic Nervous System
days.focus: The 12 cranial nerves; structural pathways of the sympathetic and parasympathetic divisions.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 6 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on cranial nerves and the ANS.

nightly: Make flashcards for all 12 cranial nerves: name, number, and sensory / motor / both.
Draw the sympathetic and parasympathetic pathways structurally.
Use a mnemonic to lock in cranial nerve order, then test yourself on a blank list.

days.focus: Gross and internal anatomy of the eye and the ear.
nightly: Label the eye and the ear in cross-section from memory.
Make flashcards for the structures light passes through and the structures sound passes through.
List the accessory structures of the eye.

days.focus: Location and gross anatomy of the major endocrine glands.

items.title: Application Activity: Endocrine Gland Locator
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Draw a body outline and place every endocrine gland in its correct location.
Make flashcards for the gross appearance and location of 8 endocrine glands.
Lab Exam 4 review: re-walk the brain, cranial nerve, senses, and endocrine stations.

days.focus: Practical exam: brain, cranial nerves, special senses, and endocrine glands.

items.title: Lab Exam 4
items.note: Covers Week 6: peripheral nervous system, senses, and endocrine anatomy.

nightly: Light night: review your Lab Exam 4 feedback once it posts.
Pre-read the heart anatomy section.
Reset your flashcard deck for the cardiovascular unit.

days.focus: Chambers, valves, great vessels, and coronary circulation anatomy.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 7 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on heart anatomy.

nightly: Draw the heart and label all four chambers, all four valves, and the great vessels.
Trace a drop of blood through the heart and label the coronary vessels.
Make flashcards for the heart valves and what each one separates.

days.focus: Major arteries and veins, structure of the three vessel wall types.
nightly: Label the major arteries and veins on a body outline from memory.
Compare the three vessel wall layers in a quick table.
Trace blood from the heart to the hand and back.

days.focus: Lymphatic vessels and organs; respiratory tract anatomy.
inClass: Application Activity: Circulatory and Airway Trace

items.title: Application Activity: Circulatory and Airway Trace
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Draw the respiratory tract from nose to alveolus and label each structure.
Map the lymphatic organs on a body outline.
Lab Exam 5 review: re-walk the heart, vessel, lymphatic, and respiratory stations.

days.focus: Practical exam: cardiovascular, lymphatic, and respiratory systems.

items.title: Lab Exam 5
items.note: Covers Week 7: cardiovascular, lymphatic, and respiratory systems.

nightly: Light night: review your Lab Exam 5 feedback once it posts.
Pre-read the digestive system section.
Begin a cumulative review list for the final exam.

days.focus: Gastrointestinal tract and accessory digestive organs anatomy.

items.title: Readiness Assurance 8 (iRAT + tRAT)
items.note: Covers pre-class reading on the digestive system.

nightly: Draw the digestive tract and label the accessory organs.
Make flashcards for the layers of the GI tract wall and the accessory organs.
Add this week's topics to your final exam review list.

days.focus: Kidney gross and internal anatomy, ureters, bladder, urethra.
nightly: Draw a kidney in frontal section and label the internal anatomy.
Trace the path of urine from the glomerulus to the urethra.
Final review: rebuild your concept map for Weeks 5 to 7.

days.focus: Male and female reproductive system anatomy.

items.title: Application Activity: Viscera Synthesis
items.note: Team 4S activity, completed and submitted in class.

nightly: Label the male and female reproductive systems from memory.
Final review: re-test every flashcard deck and retire what you have mastered.
Walk every lab station topic from the whole course one more time.

days.focus: Comprehensive lecture and lab exam covering the entire course.

items.title: Final Exam
items.note: Cumulative: all systems, lecture and lab.

nightly: You made it. Rest well before the final.
Light review only: skim your concept maps, do not cram.
Bring your student ID and arrive early.

spacedRepetition.note: Review offsets, in days, after a topic is first taught.
